body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
<!--- One sentence to describe the purpose of this PR, refer to any linked issues:
#14 -- this will link to issue 14
Closes #2 -- this will automatically close issue 2 on PR merge
-->
Adds advection, hyperdiffusion, and limiters to the EDFMX model.

## Content
<!---  specific tasks that are currently complete 
- Solution implemented
-->
- Modify the `MoistBaroclinicWaveWithEDMF` initial condition so that it sets the initial draft area to 1 and the initial TKE to 0. Add an additional `DryBaroclinicWaveWithEDMF` initial condition, which sets the same values of area and TKE.
- Add `variable_manipulations.jl`, which will contain all useful manipulations of grid-scale and sub-grid-scale variables. Move `divide_by_ρa` to this file, along with the new regularization function `sgs_weight_function`. Move `times_a` to this file, and rename it to `gs_to_sgs`. Add the additional generated functions `specific_gs`, `specific_sgs`, and `matching_subfields`, all of which need to be generated in order to avoid triggering allocations due to type inference failures (ideally, these will not need to be generated in future versions of Julia). Add other helpful functions for computing environment and draft quantities. Ensure that all functions in `variable_manipulations.jl` are thoroughly documented.
- Rename the EDFMX model's `a_min` to `a_half`, in order to reflect how `sgs_weight_function` uses this value.
- Add the specific grid-scale and sub-grid-scale states (`ᶜspecific`, `ᶜspecific⁰`, and `ᶜspecificʲs`) to the precomputed quantities. Use the specific states to greatly simplify the computation of the thermodynamic states. Comment out the modifications to `ᶜK` due to the sub-grid-scale quantities, at least until the modifications are fully detailed in the Overleaf document. Add a hacky roundoff error correction to `ᶜρʲ` in order to ensure that the EDMFX CI examples with a draft area of 1 exhibit the correct behavior (the sub-grid-scale quantities should match the grid-scale quantities when there is no precipitation). Change the diagnostic sub-grid-scale quantities to `ᶜspecific⁺`, `ᶠw⁺`, `ᶜu⁺`, `ᶠu³⁺`, `ᶜK⁺`, `ᶜts⁺`, `ᶜa⁺`, and `ᶜa⁰`. Remove all uses of `getindex` for column fields (e.g., `Y.c.sgsʲs[j]`) in `precomputed_quantities.jl`, since using this syntax involves type piracy through the `TurbulenceConvection` module, and replace them the default `getproperty` syntax instead (e.g., `Y.c.sgsʲs.:($j)`, or `@. Y.c.sgsʲs.:($$j)`).
- Add vertical advection of sub-grid-scale values to `implicit_tendency!`. Rewrite `vertical_transport!` so that it uses the precomputed specific states. Ensure that every quantity in the specific states is vertically transported, with special cases for total energy (since it corresponds to the transport of total enthalpy). Add `edmfx_advection`, and use it for the vertical transport of sub-grid-scale variables. Set its default value to `:none` (central differencing).
- Add advection of sub-grid-scale values to `horizontal_advection_tendency!` and `explicit_vertical_advection_tendency!`. Move the horizontal advection of tracers to `horizontal_tracer_advection_tendency!` in `advection.jl`, and add horizontal advection of sub-grid-scale tracers.
- Add hyperdiffusion of sub-grid-scale values to `hyerdiffusion_tendency!` and `tracer_hyperdiffusion_tendency!`. As with the implicit tendency, rewrite this tendency in terms of the specific states, and ensure that every quantity in the specific states gets hyperdiffusion applied to it. Note that TKE is treated as energy, not as a tracer, so it is affected in `hyerdiffusion_tendency!`.
- Add limiting of sub-grid-scale variables to `limiters_func!`.
- Remove the computation of the nonexistent diagnostic quantities `ᶜq_rai⁰`, `ᶜq_sno⁰`, `ᶜq_rai⁺`, and `ᶜq_sno⁺` for `Microphysics1Moment`.
- Ensure that the modified tendency functions do not trigger allocations due to type inference failures, lowering the allocation limits in `flame.jl`.
- Modify the EDMFX CI examples to use an explicit timestepper (`SSP33ShuOsher`), so that it is not necessary to modify the implicit solver for sub-grid-scale values. Reduce the values of `dt`, `dt_save_to_disk`, and `t_end` to account for the fact that the grid-scale tendencies are no longer treated implicitly. Create a copy of the CI example that runs `MoistBaroclinicWaveWithEDMF` with the EDMFX model, but don't give it a precipitation model. Since precipitation currently only affects grid-scale values, it breaks the symmetry between the grid-scale and sub-grid-scale values. Add another CI example that runs `DryBaroclinicWaveWithEDMF` with the EDFMX model. Ensure that the last two examples have identical evolution of grid-scale and sub-grid-scale values, up to roundoff error.
- Increment the ref counter, since refactoring the tendency functions changes the order of floating point operations, introducing a small MSE relative to the references on the main branch.

<!---
Review checklist

I have:
- followed the codebase contribution guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/Contributing/
- followed the style guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/DevDocs/CodeStyle/
- followed the documentation policy: https://github.com/CliMA/policies/wiki/Documentation-Policy
- checked that this PR does not duplicate an open PR.

In the Content, I have included 
- relevant unit tests, and integration tests, 
- appropriate docstrings on all functions, structs, and modules, and included relevant documentation.

-->

----
- [x] I have read and checked the items on the review checklist.
title: Add advection, hyperdiffusion, and limiters to EDMFX model
